auth.usage_instructions: Browser-based authentication for DoorDash. Login opens a browser window for manual login. Session is saved for subsequent commands.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Login via browser. Idempotent -- skips if already logged in. Use --force/-F to clear existing session and re-authenticate.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Remove saved browser session and credentials. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `doordash auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `doordash auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `doordash auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `doordash auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Run `doordash auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test`` (API/OAuth) or via the live browser check (browser_session).
status.usage_instructions: Check if authenticated. Exit code 0 = authenticated, 2 = not authenticated.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Validate the saved session by launching a browser and checking auth state. Use --verbose/-v for detailed checks. Exit code 0 = OK, 2 = not authenticated.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `doordash cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `doordash cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

orders.usage_instructions: View and inspect DoorDash orders, and re-place prior orders. List recent orders with filtering, get details for a specific order by ID, or reorder a past order via browser automation.

get.help: Get details for a specific order.
get.usage_instructions: Get full details for a single order by ORDER_ID (positional argument).

list.usage_instructions: List recent DoorDash orders. Use --filter/-f for field:op:value filtering (e.g. current_status:eq:delivered, store_name:contains:Pizza). Use --properties/-p to select fields. Default limit is 100.

reorder.help: Reorder a previous DoorDash order. Default is dry-run (no charge).
reorder.usage_instructions: Re-place a previous DoorDash order. Default behavior is a safe dry run that calls the `reorderOrder` GraphQL mutation server-side to build a cart, then prints the cart UUID, store, items, and item subtotal -- no browser is launched. Pass --confirm to additionally open a browser, navigate to /checkout?orderCartId=<cart>, and click Place Order (this charges the saved payment method). --confirm and --dry-run cannot be combined. The order argument accepts either a merchant order id (e.g. 3000010020128114) or the canonical order UUID; numeric ids are resolved against history (up to 1000 past orders). The mutation is idempotent: calling it twice for the same order returns the same cart UUID rather than creating duplicates.

arguments.name: order_id
arguments.help: Order ID or UUID to reorder

options.name: --confirm
options.help: Actually place the order (charges saved payment)

stores.usage_instructions: Browse restaurants and stores available for delivery near the saved address.

get.help: Get a store from the current store feed by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Run `doordash stores get --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List available stores/restaurants near your saved delivery address.
list.usage_instructions: List restaurants near your saved delivery address. Use --filter/-f for filtering (e.g. is_open:eq:true, rating:gte:4). Default limit is 50.

usage_instructions: Use for interacting with DoorDash via browser automation. Commands follow `doordash <group> <action>` pattern. Use --no-cache to bypass response cache. Requires browser auth via `doordash auth login` first. To re-place a prior order use `doordash orders reorder <ORDER_ID>` (dry run by default; pass --confirm to actually charge the saved payment method).
